summary: Implemented deterministic, versioned, authoritative M6 policy/event runtime substrate. Policy/event definitions now pass explicit content schema/runtime parsing and controlled sim-core boot consumption; scheduler activates due events deterministically; option resolution uses the shared GameCommand path for player and AI; save/load preserves and rejects malformed policy/event slices.

tests_run.result: 1 file passed, 5 tests passed; covers malformed definition injection rejection, controlled boot, deterministic event ordering, player/AI command parity, and save/load malformed-slice rejection.
Node and worker-compatible hashes matched for hello, command/query, save/load, M4 replay, and M5 playable-loop canaries.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism canaries passed; root vitest 38 files/292 tests passed; web vitest 1 file/4 tests passed.

decisions: Added explicit M6 policy/event schema/runtime data instead of scripting, callbacks, reflection, or object-graph serialization.
Kept authoritative mutation inside sim-core commit paths: daily scheduler activation and sim.choose-policy-event-option command resolution.
Kept player and AI parity by adding one shared GameCommand; actor is emitted in DomainEvent but not embedded into WorldState, preserving equal state hashes for equivalent choices.
Versioned M6 policy/event runtime save slice with reason-coded schema rejection for malformed save payloads.
Marked task REVIEW only; did not mark ACCEPTED/CLOSED and did not merge.
risks: R3: WorldState, scheduler, protocol, and save-format were changed and need systems_architect review before QA.
This is runtime substrate only; M7 formal content quality/content lock remains out of scope.
No production scenario content was added; tests use synthetic fixtures under tests.
requested_action: Review the R3 policy/event runtime substrate for architecture, save/protocol compatibility, deterministic scheduler behavior, and controlled boot-boundary constraints; then route onward per reviewer decision.
